intro.title: The Black Ships to Ilios
intro.body: The Catalogue of Ships in Iliad II names roughly 1,186 vessels mustering at Aulis under Agamemnon to recover Helen from Troy. The siege runs ten years; only the final year is sung. The route shown is the bridgehead, not the campaign — Aulis to Tenedos to the Trojan plain at Hisarlık.

stops.name: Aulis
stops.note: Mustering harbour on the Euboean Gulf. Iliad II describes the fleet held there by contrary winds until Iphigenia's sacrifice released them.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured cartographic plate, muted ink. Wide bay packed with low black-hulled triremes, lateen sails furled, smoke from an altar on the headland, weathered antique colour palette.

stops.name: Tenedos
stops.note: Island staging-point off the Troad. Apollo's island in the Iliad; later the Greeks pretend to withdraw here before the wooden horse.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured cartographic plate, muted ink. Small forested island seen from the sea, beached ships drawn up on the strand, smoke of cookfires, sepia and umber tones.

stops.name: Troy (Hisarlık)
stops.note: Citadel mound at Hisarlık, identified by Schliemann in 1870. The Iliadic plain stretches between citadel and the Hellespont; the Scamander and Simois rivers cross it.
stops.imagePrompt: Hand-coloured cartographic plate, muted ink. Citadel walls of cyclopean stone on a low mound, plain beyond with two rivers, distant beached ships, ochre and slate palette.
